assertion_results.text: Step 4.4 searches for preemptive tasks using JQL with labels 'security-preemptive' and 'CVE-2026-55123' (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: reconciliation.md lines 13-16 show the JQL query: "project = TC AND issuetype = Task AND labels = 'security-preemptive' AND labels = 'CVE-2026-55123' ORDER BY created DESC". This searches for tasks with both the 'security-preemptive' and 'CVE-2026-55123' labels. triage-outcome.md line 61 confirms: "A JQL search for tasks with labels `security-preemptive` and `CVE-2026-55123` returned TC-8022".

assertion_results.text: Step 4.4 filters results to match the current stream (rhtpa-2.1) by checking the task summary for the stream name
assertion_results.evidence: reconciliation.md lines 20-22 under '2. Filter to current stream': "TC-8022 summary contains `(rhtpa-2.1)` which matches the current issue's stream suffix `[rhtpa-2.1]`. This is a matching preemptive task for this stream." This explicitly shows filtering by checking the task summary for the stream name.

assertion_results.text: Step 4.4 links the new CVE Jira TC-8021 to the preemptive task TC-8022 with 'Depend' link type (standard remediation linkage)
assertion_results.evidence: reconciliation.md lines 38-48 show the link creation: "jira.create_link(inwardIssue: "TC-8021", outwardIssue: "TC-8022", type: "Depend")" and describes it as "the standard remediation linkage between the CVE Jira (TC-8021) and the remediation task (TC-8022)". triage-outcome.md line 64 confirms: "Linked TC-8021 to TC-8022 with link type "Depend" (standard remediation linkage)".

assertion_results.text: Step 4.4 removes the 'security-preemptive' label from TC-8022 — the task is now a standard remediation task linked to a proper CVE Jira
assertion_results.evidence: reconciliation.md lines 52-61 show the label removal: labels updated from ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"] to ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123"], and states: "The `security-preemptive` label is removed because the task is now linked to a proper CVE Jira (TC-8021). The task transitions from being a preemptive/proactive task to a standard remediation task." triage-outcome.md line 65 confirms the same.

assertion_results.text: Step 7 skips remediation task creation for this stream because Step 4.4 reconciliation already linked an existing task
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md lines 76-78 under 'Step 7 -- Remediation': "Outcome: No new remediation tasks created. Because Step 4.4 reconciled the existing preemptive task TC-8022 with this CVE Jira, Step 7 skips task creation for stream 2.1.x." Also reconciliation.md line 80: "Remediation already exists for stream 2.1.x via TC-8022. Step 7 will skip task creation for this stream." And triage-outcome.md line 99: "New tasks created | None -- existing preemptive task reconciled".
